# Performance Requirements Quality Checklist: Website Improvements

**Purpose**: Validate that performance requirements (LCP, CLS, image optimization, layout shift, reduced-motion) are complete, quantified, and verifiable. Tests the quality of what is specified, not implementation behavior.  
**Created**: 2026-02-28  
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are the exact LCP and CLS threshold values (e.g. 2.5s, 0.1) specified in the spec or only given as examples in parentheses? [Completeness, Spec §FR-013, User Story 5 Acceptance 1]
- [ ] CHK002 Is the set of "critical routes" for performance targets explicitly listed (e.g. homepage, About, writing index, one writing page, projects index)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-013, Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK003 Are requirements specified for how "when the host supports image optimization" is determined so implementers know when to apply FR-013? [Completeness, Spec §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK004 Is "optimized for the requesting device" defined (e.g. responsive srcset, format, size) or left to implementation? [Completeness, Spec §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK005 Are "fonts and above-the-fold content" that must not cause layout shift explicitly identified (e.g. hero, headings, main prose)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-014]
- [ ] CHK006 Is "reduced-motion preference" required to be respected for all motion (hero, section reveal) or only specific components? [Completeness, Spec §FR-014]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK007 Is "largest contentful paint" tied to a specific metric or standard (e.g. Core Web Vitals LCP) so measurement is unambiguous? [Clarity, Spec §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK008 Is "layout stability" or "layout shift" tied to a specific metric (e.g. CLS) and threshold so it can be measured? [Clarity, Spec §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK009 Is "noticeable layout shift" defined (e.g. CLS below 0.1, or qualitative "no visible jump") so FR-014 is testable? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014]
- [ ] CHK010 Is "where motion is used" enumerated (e.g. hero animation, section reveal) so reduced-motion requirements apply consistently? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK011 Do performance targets in the spec align with the plan (e.g. "LCP ≤ 2.5s, CLS ≤ 0.1") so there is a single source of truth? [Consistency, Spec §FR-013, Plan]
- [ ] CHK012 Is the Edge Case "image optimization not available" (images still served, targets met where possible) reflected in a requirement so conditional behavior is clear? [Consistency, Spec §FR-013, Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK013 Are "critical routes" for performance (FR-013) the same set as for automation (FR-019) or is the difference intentional? [Consistency, Spec §FR-013, FR-019]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK014 Can SC-006 ("critical routes meet defined LCP and layout stability targets in production or representative test environment") be verified without implementation details? [Measurability, Spec §SC-006]
- [ ] CHK015 Is the measurement environment (e.g. production, Lighthouse CI, device/network) specified for performance acceptance? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 5, SC-006]
- [ ] CHK016 Can "no noticeable layout shift from webfont or hero" be objectively tested (e.g. CLS threshold, or visual regression)? [Measurability, Spec §FR-014]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK017 Are performance requirements defined for both homepage and writing page (and optionally other critical routes) or only "e.g."? [Coverage, Spec §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK018 When image optimization is not supported by the host, are requirements specified for acceptable fallback (e.g. unoptimized serve, no build failure)? [Coverage, Spec §FR-013, Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK019 Are requirements specified for first load vs navigation (e.g. both must meet targets, or only first load)? [Coverage, Gap]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK020 Is the "image optimization not available" scenario explicitly required (images usable, targets met where possible) in a functional requirement or only in Edge Cases narrative? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK021 Are performance degradation requirements defined when third-party or optional content (e.g. GitHub graph) is slow or fails? [Edge Case, Gap]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK022 Is the assumption that "hosting environment may or may not support image optimization" documented so FR-013 conditional is unambiguous? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK023 Are performance targets required to be documented in a single place (e.g. plan, constants) so they stay consistent with CI and acceptance? [Consistency, Spec §FR-013]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- These items validate the quality of performance requirements as written; they do not verify that the site meets performance targets.
- Each `/speckit.checklist` run creates a new file; this file focuses on performance only.
